channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 PC Specs Look Pretty Permissive
item.content:encoded:
Playground Games and Xbox have announced the Forza Horizon 6 PC specs list, and if you just want to play the game without too many bells and whistles, the floor is surprisingly low.

In a message on X, the studios reveal four presets for the open-world simcade racer ahead of its release in May. As a reminder, the game will be landing on PC and Xbox Series X|S first, with a PS5 version also in the works for later on.

As you'd expect, the Minimum preset isn't going to be asking too much of you and your PC, but for the Extreme RT (ray tracing) preset, you're going to want something that's a bit more capable.

Here's a full list of Forza Horizon 6's PC specs, arranged according to preset.

Minimum (Low preset, 1080p, 60fps)

  • CPU: Intel Core i5-8400 or AMD Ryzen 5 1600
  • GPU: Nvidia GeForce GTX 1650, AMD Radeon RX 6500 XT, or Intel Arc A380
  • 16GB RAM
  • SSD storage
  • Windows 10 or 11, version 22H2

Recommended (High preset, 1440p, 60+fps)

  • CPU: Intel Core i5-12400F or AMD Ryzen 5 5600X
  • GPU: Nvidia GeForce RTX 3060 Ti, AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT, or Intel Arc A580
  • 16GB RAM
  • SSD storage
  • Windows 10 or 11, version 22H2

Extreme (Extreme preset, 4K, 60+fps)

  • CPU: Intel Core i7-12700K or AMD Ryzen 7 7700X
  • GPU: Nvidia GeForce RTX 4070 Ti or AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT
  • 24GB RAM
  • NVMe SSD storage
  • Windows 10 or 11, version 22H2

Extreme RT (Extreme RT preset, 4K upscaled, 60+fps)

  • CPU: Intel Core i7-12700K or AMD Ryzen 7 7700X
  • GPU: Nvidia GeForce RTX 5070 Ti or AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT
  • 32GB RAM
  • NVMe SSD storage
  • Windows 10 or 11, version 22H2

As you can see, these PC specs aren't too demanding, although as you ascend through the ranks, you're going to need a beefier machine to keep up. Such is the way with PC gaming.

Forza Horizon 6 is due to launch on PC and Xbox Series X|S on May 19th, and a version for PS5 is coming somewhere down the line as well.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
